channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Sintopia Release Date Puts Hell in Your Hands in April
item.content:encoded:
Developer Piraknights Games and publisher Team17 have announced the Sintopia release date, with the hellish management sim wreaking havoc on PC very soon indeed.

As revealed in a new release date trailer, Sintopia will be arriving on PC on April 16th. Unfortunately, there's no release date for consoles, so it looks like you're going to have to fire up your computer if you want to play this one.

Originally announced back in October 2024, Sintopia lets you manage Hell itself, tending to sinners and the unworthy as they descend into the underworld. It'll be up to you to decide just how well Hell is run and to build an environment that's conducive to maximum punishment for the guilty.

Sintopia was initially poised for an Early Access release, but back in August last year, Piraknights and Team17 decided that they would both delay the game's release and launch it as a full game when it did eventually arrive.

As well as a fully-voiced campaign, Sintopia will also come complete with a sandbox mode that lets you tweak Hell's parameters however you like in order to create whatever sort of experience you desire.

The game will be split into two segments: the Overworld, where you'll recruit new sinners for your cause, and Hell, which you'll rule over as you see fit. The two will complement each other, so you'll need to be across both if you want to thrive.

Naturally, the seven deadly sins will also be making an appearance, and per Piraknights, they'll be "wielding their own special powers to try and take over your settlement". Best be on your guard.

Sintopia launches on PC via Steam on April 16th, and it won't be the Early Access release that was initially announced, so you should be getting the whole shebang with this one. Stay tuned for more.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph
